new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource using specific COVID-19 vaccine codes and the patient's ID, rather than assuming no records exist based on a limited search. You must explicitly state the `occurrenceDateTime` of the most recent vaccine found in the record and compare it to the 12-month threshold before concluding whether a new booster order is necessary.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must perform the necessary tool calls to discontinue any identified duplicate orders immediately, rather than just identifying them in your response. Ensure you use the `update` or `edit` tool to set the status of all but the newest order to 'discontinued' so that exactly one active order remains in the patient's record.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must perform a comprehensive search of all active `MedicationRequest` resources, specifically checking the pharmacological class of every medication to identify opioids, as the agent failed to recognize that even common medications can be opioids. You must explicitly list all active opioid medications found to justify your conclusion and verify the presence of a corresponding naloxone order for each, as failing to identify these agents leads to a false negative result regarding the need for co-prescribed naloxone.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must perform a comprehensive search of all active `MedicationRequest` resources and verify the drug against a complete list of opioid ingredients and pharmacological classes (e.g., morphine derivatives, fentanyl analogs, codeine, tramadol), as the agent incorrectly excluded active opioids from its assessment. You must explicitly list every active opioid identified in the record and confirm the presence of a corresponding naloxone prescription for each to ensure patient safety and avoid false negative results.

when asked to calculate average heart rate over specific time intervals, you must query the `Observation` resource using the standard LOINC code `8867-4` (Heart Rate) and the `date` parameter (e.g., `date=ge[timestamp]`) to restrict results to the requested 6-hour and 12-hour lookback windows. You must parse the JSON response to extract the specific observation values, list them in your response, and perform the arithmetic mean calculation, rather than returning the raw API call or failing to process the data.

when asked to determine the date of a patient's last influenza vaccine, you must search specifically for `Immunization` resources using influenza-related vaccine codes (e.g., 90674, 90686) rather than assuming no records exist based on a generic search. You must explicitly state the `occurrenceDateTime` of the most recent vaccine found in the `Immunization` history to justify your decision, and only proceed to order a new vaccine if your search confirms that the last dose was administered more than 365 days ago or if no prior vaccination record exists.

when asked to calculate average heart rate over specific time intervals, you must query the `Observation` resource for the patient using the `category=vital-signs` and `code=8867-4` (Heart Rate) parameters, as a search restricted only to a specific "flowsheet" name may fail to retrieve data. If an initial search by LOINC code returns no results, you must verify the patient ID and broaden the search parameters to include all `vital-signs` category observations before concluding that no data exists.

when asked to determine the date of a patient's last influenza vaccine, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource using influenza-specific vaccine codes (e.g., 90674, 90686, 90688) instead of relying on generic searches or assuming no records exist based on a failure to find a `Procedure` resource. You must explicitly state the `occurrenceDateTime` of the most recent vaccine found in the `Immunization` history and compare it to the 365-day threshold to justify whether a new order is necessary.

when asked to check for a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours, you must search for all active `Device` resources, not just `Procedure` or `ServiceRequest` resources, to ensure the catheter is not identified as an active device. You must explicitly state the insertion date and time found in the `Device` record to justify the calculation of the duration.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource using the patient ID, but you must not assume that a lack of records necessitates a new order without verifying the clinical context. If no records are found, you must state that no evidence of prior vaccination exists, but verify if the patient is eligible or requires an initial series rather than automatically issuing a "booster" (CPT 91320), which is specifically for patients who have already completed a primary series.

If the search returns no results, you must verify the patient's ID and search for all `Immunization` resources before concluding that no vaccine history exists.
